content.verdict: Château de Feuilles is the hidden gem of the Seychelles honeymoon circuit — a French-owned boutique property with just 10 rooms perched on a hilltop at the northern tip of Praslin. The views over the turquoise waters toward Curieuse and Cousin islands are genuinely extraordinary, especially at sunset. With fewer than 20 guests on property at any time, the atmosphere is closer to a private house than a hotel. The French-Creole cooking is outstanding — regularly cited as some of the best food in the Seychelles — and the chef takes real pride in local ingredients. It is not a beach-at-the-door property; the descent to the beach takes a few minutes. Best for: couples who prioritise atmosphere, cooking, and views over beach access. Skip it if: you want to roll out of bed directly onto sand.
content.best_room: Book the Panoramic Suite — the largest category, with a wraparound terrace giving 270-degree views over the northern Seychelles. Request the unit on the highest level for maximum elevation above the treetops. The pool villas are also excellent but the Panoramic Suite's terrace is the reason you come here. Ask for a room away from the kitchen building.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Praslin Arrival & Hilltop Sundowners
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly from Mahé to Praslin on Air Seychelles (15 minutes) or take the Cat Cocos ferry (1 hour). Transfer to the Château — the arrival by winding road through endemic Praslin forest sets the tone perfectly. Check in, unpack, and walk to the terrace for sunset with a rum sour. The view on a clear evening toward Curieuse is among the best in the Seychelles.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Vallée de Mai — UNESCO Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Vallée de Mai, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and home to the famous Coco de Mer palm, is about 15 minutes from the hotel. Go early morning (opens at 8am) before tour groups arrive. The ancient palm forest feels genuinely prehistoric. Afternoon at the hotel pool, which has some of the best hilltop views of any pool in Seychelles.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Anse Lazio Beach Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Anse Lazio on Praslin's northwest tip is consistently rated among the top 10 beaches in the world — large granite boulders, powder white sand, and electric-blue water. Take a taxi there by 9am before day-trippers arrive from other islands. The snorkeling at the rocky ends of the beach is outstanding. Lunch at the beachside restaurant before returning to the Château for a French-inspired dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Curieuse Island Excursion
itinerary_7_nights.description: Book a half-day boat excursion to Curieuse Island, a national marine park with giant Aldabra tortoises roaming freely on the beach — an extraordinary and intimate wildlife encounter. The surrounding water is clear with good snorkeling. Return by lunchtime. Afternoon in-villa reading, followed by a tasting menu dinner at the Château's restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: La Digue Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take the inter-island ferry from Praslin to La Digue (15 minutes). Rent bicycles and cycle to Anse Source d'Argent — the most photographed beach in the Indian Ocean, with its otherworldly pink granite boulders. La Digue is wonderfully car-free and slow-paced. Return to Praslin on the late afternoon ferry for a romantic hilltop dinner at the Château.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa & Slow Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Book the couples massage at the on-site spa. The treatment room has views over the ocean — one of the more memorable spa settings in the archipelago. Spend the afternoon at the hotel pool. Ask the chef to prepare a private dinner on the main terrace for your last evening — the kitchen is happy to accommodate special requests with 24 hours notice.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A slow breakfast on the terrace watching the ocean lighten in the morning sun. If your flight is in the afternoon, you have time to revisit Anse Lazio for a final swim. Praslin Airport is small and relaxed — arrive 90 minutes before domestic or international departure.

caveats: The beach is not directly at the hotel — you need a short drive or walk. If lying on the sand steps from your room is the priority, this is not the right pick.
Ten rooms means this property books out many months in advance for peak periods (December–January, July–August). Book as early as possible.
The hilltop location means no air conditioning is needed in most seasons — but the trade-off is that the property can feel warm in March and the SE wind months. Confirm room ventilation.
Praslin has limited nightlife and shopping. This is very much a retreat — wonderful if that is what you want, underwhelming if you wanted variety.
content.hotel_email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon Reservation — [Your Names] — [Check-in Date]

Dear Château de Feuilles Team,

My partner and I are honeymooning at your property from [check-in] to [check-out] (Booking reference: [reference]).

We would be very grateful for the following:
1. The Panoramic Suite if available, positioned at the highest level for the best views — or the best ocean-view room in our booked category
2. We would love a bottle of local Takamaka rum and fresh tropical fruit waiting on arrival
3. We would like to reserve a private terrace dinner on [date] — could you let us know what the chef recommends for that evening and whether a special menu is possible?

Thank you and we are very much looking forward to our stay.

Warm regards,
[Your Names]

faqs.question: Is Chateau de Feuilles good for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Very much so — the intimate scale (10 rooms), exceptional French-Creole cuisine, and extraordinary hilltop views over northern Praslin make it a deeply romantic property. It suits couples who prioritize atmosphere and food over beach-at-the-door convenience. The 4.9 TripAdvisor rating reflects a near-flawless guest experience.

faqs.question: Best room for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: The Panoramic Suite, on the highest level with a wraparound terrace and 270-degree ocean views. Ask for the unit furthest from the kitchen building. The pool villas are the runner-up if the suite is booked. In any category, request an ocean-facing aspect — the views are the signature feature of this property.

faqs.question: Best time to visit the Seychelles?
faqs.answer: April–May and October–November are ideal — warm, calm seas, and fewer crowds. Praslin's northwest beaches like Anse Lazio are sheltered during the SE trade winds (June–August) but can have rougher surf on the southwest. December–January is busiest and most expensive; shoulder season offers the same conditions at better rates.

faqs.question: Which island is Chateau de Feuilles on and how do you get there?
faqs.answer: Château de Feuilles is on Praslin, the Seychelles' second-largest island. Fly from Mahé to Praslin Airport on Air Seychelles (15 minutes, several daily departures) or take the Cat Cocos catamaran ferry (about 1 hour). The hotel arranges transfers from both the airport and ferry terminal.

faqs.question: What's the snorkeling/diving like?
faqs.answer: Snorkeling is excellent nearby: the rocky ends of Anse Lazio have diverse reef fish, and the marine park around Curieuse offers sea turtles and healthy coral. The hotel can arrange boat excursions. For scuba diving, Praslin has dive operators offering trips to the Sisters and Booby Island for shark and manta encounters.

faqs.question: Insider tip for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Request a private dinner on the main terrace specifically on a clear evening — the view of the lights of La Digue and the surrounding islands at night is magical. Ask when booking and remind them on arrival. The kitchen is very accommodating for special menus; a 24-hour heads-up on any dietary preferences transforms the meal.

true_cost_breakdown.item: Flights to Seychelles (per couple, from Europe/US)
Inter-island transfers (Mahé to Praslin, flights + transfers)
